creator: bugzilla.freebsd@omnilan.de
text:
(In reply to Andre Albsmeier from comment #42)
> I tried all of your config stuff (as long as it seemed reasonable) and had
> a 90 degrees turned output :-) but no accel mode after VT-switch.
>
> Which xorg-server do you use? An official pkg or selfmade from ports?

Selfmade packages from ports.

_Interesting surprise_ yesterday, after 'pkg upgrade' here:
xdriifno
"Screen 0:" read 'zink' and the "authenticate magic 1"/"failed to load driver: iris" errors occurred.
'zink' was in use to my surprise, and the "authenticate magic 1"/"failed to load driver: iris" was back (can't remember why I utilized xdriinfo(1), nothing was broken otherwise).

I didn't change any config, and the driver still was the same after the 'pkg upgrade'
(drm-61-kmod-6.1.128 with libdrm-2.4.123,1) but I didn't recognize that gpu-firmware-intel-kmod was removed!
Today I reinstalled gpu-firmware-intel-kmod(-kabylake-20230625.1402504) and now xdriinfo(1) shows 'iris' again.

So the config extension 'Option      "DRI"       "3"' I reported only has the desired effect if the korrekt gpu-firmware-intel-kmod is loaded.
I'm also surprised that x11 works without at all...
But gpu-firmware-intel-kmod is crucial to our topic.

One day I hopefully will have time to get a better grip on the big picture and write something down more helpful than some accidental findings...

Aftre stripping timestamps, I don't see any difference in Xorg.0.log whether gpu-firmware-intel-kmod was loaded or not.
Bu I noticed these:
(II) AIGLX: Suspending AIGLX clients for VT switch
(II) AIGLX: Resuming AIGLX clients after VT switch

I can't reproduce the post-vt-switch 'Accelerated: no' problem here (glxinfo -B | grep -i accel).  Are these log lines related to that problem, i.e. are they logged on problematic systems too?


Like mentioned, the log of my last x11 session without kmod-firmware was exactly the same as the log from today, with kmod-firmware loaded.
Here's my timestamp stripped and condensed log (grep -v -e event7 -e modeline /tmp/Xorg.0.log.notimes | xz -c | uuencode -m Xorg.DRIOK.log.xz):
